title: The Water Treaty
premise: The upstream nation of Asoria has completed a new dam that drastically reduces water flow to the downstream nation of Beloria, which is in the grips of a severe drought. You are a mediator from an international body, and neither side fully trusts you. Farmers, generals, and upcoming elections pressure both governments to act or concede.
setting: The Aritan River basin, a semi-arid region. Asoria is a rapidly industrializing nation that built the dam for hydroelectric power and irrigation; Beloria is an agrarian country whose crops and livestock depend on the river. Negotiations take place in a neutral conference room in a border town, while protests and military posturing escalate outside.
goal: Reach a binding water-sharing agreement between Asoria and Beloria that ensures minimum flow to Beloria while allowing Asoria to operate its dam. Success: both sides sign a treaty and de-escalate. Failure: talks collapse, leading to water riots, crop failure in Beloria, or military confrontation.
gm_notes: Introduce complications: a hardliner general in Beloria wants to blow up the dam; Asoria's president faces re-election and cannot appear weak; an international corporation has invested in Asoria's dam. The mediator might discover a secret Asorian plan to divert water for a mining project. Escalate pressure each round: protests grow, water rations tighten, election deadlines approach.

roles.name: Mediator
roles.description: An impartial diplomat from the International Water Commission tasked with brokering a fair deal.
roles.private_info: You have intelligence that a militant group in Beloria is planning to sabotage the dam, which could trigger war. You are also aware that the head of the Asorian water authority has a personal stake in the dam's success due to family investments.

roles.name: Asorian Lead Negotiator
roles.description: The pragmatic but pressured chief negotiator for Asoria, under orders to protect the dam's output.
roles.private_info: Your president has secretly authorized you to offer a 10% flow increase in exchange for Belorian non-interference, but you must extract trade concessions first. You also know that the dam's reservoir is lower than reported due to a leak.

roles.name: Belorian Lead Negotiator
roles.description: The determined and desperate representative of Beloria, whose country is running out of drinking water.
roles.private_info: Your military intelligence has confirmed the dam's structural vulnerability, and you have a plan to threaten exposure unless immediate water release is granted. Your president demands a minimum 30% flow, but you could settle for 20% if Asoria helps with cloud seeding.

npcs.name: General Okafor
npcs.description: Belorian army chief, a stern man with a hawkish reputation.
npcs.hidden_agenda: He wants the dam destroyed to restore the river's flow, and is secretly funneling support to saboteurs to force a crisis.

npcs.name: President Karina Delacroix
npcs.description: Asorian president, a poised leader seeking a strong legacy.
npcs.hidden_agenda: She needs a treaty to secure re-election, but will accept nothing less than 70% water retention; she also hopes to sell surplus water to a neighboring desert nation for profit.

npcs.name: Father Emilio
npcs.description: Belorian village priest and grassroots leader organizing water protests.
npcs.hidden_agenda: He is peaceful but radicalized; he intends to lead a non-violent blockade of the dam to draw international media attention.

npcs.name: CEO Akira Tanaka
npcs.description: CEO of Aritan HydroCorp, the company that built the dam.
npcs.hidden_agenda: His company has invested heavily in the dam and collateralized it with international banks; any reduction in output could bankrupt him, so he lobbies Asoria to hold firm.

npcs.name: Ambassador Layla Hamid
npcs.hidden_agenda: Under pressure from her home country, she wants the treaty to include a provision that opens the region for a multinational pipeline project that would bypass both nations.
